title: salix × undulata var. hippophaefolia
intro: Salix × undulata var. hippophaefolia, also known as Hippophae or Sea Buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a hybrid of the Tatarian willow (Salix ×sepulcralis) and the Sea Buckthorn (Hippophae rhamnoides). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Salix × undulata var. hippophaefolia include improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and protection against oxidative stress, as well as potential benefits for skin and eye health. This plant is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and extracts, which can be consumed as dietary supplements or applied topically for skin care and wound healing.
